sections.paragraph:
m. pl. -ִים and ֹות (from the root עָגַל to roll)-

(1) a track, or rut in which the wheels revolve, Psa 65:12.

(2) a way, Psa 140:6 Pro 2:18. Often metaph. (like דֶּרֶךְ, נְתִיבָה) used of course of action, Psa 23:3 Pro 2:9, 15 4:26 .

מעגל

m. 1Sa 26:5, 1Sa 26:7, and מַעְגָּלָה fem. (denom. from עֶגְלָה a wagon), a wagon rampart, a fortification constructed of the wagons and other baggage of the army, 1Sa 17:20.
